MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
MWVencEncode C++

The key features are as follows:

  1. capturing and rendering videos using APIs in Universal Functions, Pro Capture Family Functions and Eco Capture Family Functions.
  2. hardware coding the captured data using APIs in Hardware Encoder Module Functions.
  3. support for H264 and H265 encoding based on Nvidia, Intel, AMD hardware.
  4. writing the encoded content into a file which can be played via ffplay.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\MWVencEncode

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family, Eco Capture Family, and USB Capture Family

Feature

  1. Preview captured video.
  2. The captured data is encoded as H264 or H265, and written as a file through hardware coding.
  3. Switch capture channel.

Calling logic

The main thread:
  1. Initialize Dear ImGui.
  2. To initialize and enumerate all available devices, , call: MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount
  3. To create video renderer, call: CMWOpenGLRender
  4. Enter the main thread loop.
  5. Click the Close button, and exit the application.
Video capture thread

1. Pro Capture Family

  1. To start video capture, call: MWStartVideoCapture.
  2. To register a timer notification, call: MWRegisterTimer.
  3. To Set the timer to trigger one frame of video data capturing, call: MWScheduleTimer, MWCaptureVideoFrameToVirtualAddressEx.
  4. To get the current capture status and release the capture card resources, call:: MWGetVideoCaptureStatus.
  5. To process captured video data, call: OnCaptureCallback.
  6. To close the current active channel and change to preview the new signal, call: OnVideoSignalChanged.
  7. Repeat step 3 to 6, until the thread quits.
  8. To quit thread and clear resources, call: MWUnregisterNotify, MWUnregisterTimer

2. Eco Capture Family

  1. To start video capture, call: MWStartVideoEcoCapture.
  2. To set video data storage space, call: MWCaptureSetVideoEcoFrame.
  3. To one frame of video data, call: MWGetVideoEcoCaptureStatus.
  4. To render captured video data, call: OnCaptureCallback.
  5. To reset the frame storage space, call: MWCaptureSetVideoEcoFrame.
  6. To close the current active channel and change to preview the new signal, call: OnVideoSignalChanged.
  7. Repeat step 3 to 6, until the thread quits.
  8. To quit thread and clear resources, call: MWUnregisterNotify, MWStopVideoEcoCapture

3. USB Capture Family

  1. To start video capture, call: MWCreateVideoCapture
  2. To stop capture, call: MWDestoryVideoCapture

Video encoding

  1. To create a renderer, call: mw_venc_create_by_index
  2. To import data to be encoded, call: mw_venc_put_frame
  3. Callback to get the encoded data.
  4. To destory encoders, call: mw_venc_destory
